alerts.title: Trump claims Iran retains only 21% of missile capacity after US airstrikes
alerts.summary: Trump administration asserts Iran's ballistic missile stockpile has been reduced to 21-22% of pre-conflict levels following recent US strikes on Iranian radar and telecommunications facilities. Claims appear designed to signal degradation of Iranian strike capability and underscore US military superiority. Statement from Jerusalem Post and Anadolu Ajans suggests either propaganda messaging or genuine assessment of Iranian losses—critical for understanding remaining Iranian conventional threat.

alerts.title: Iran fires 7 ballistic missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain; US intercepts 6
alerts.summary: Iran launched 7 ballistic missiles targeting Kuwait airport and Bahrain on 6 June. CENTCOM reports 6 missiles intercepted and 1 failed to reach target. Attack killed 1 person and injured 63 at Kuwait airport per Guardian and CBS News. Escalation occurs despite active ceasefire negotiations, marking direct Iranian response to earlier US strikes on Sirik and Qeshm Island facilities.

alerts.title: Houthis escalate Red Sea attacks; Iran pressuring missile and drone strikes
alerts.summary: Houthis conducting multiple attacks on Red Sea shipping and Israel, with reports indicating Iran is pressuring group to target oil tankers. Four separate incidents reported killing 3 crew members and destroying vessels. Attacks threaten global shipping routes and signal Iranian coordination with proxies despite ceasefire talks. Red Sea operational tempo increasing per AP News, BBC, France 24.

alerts.title: IAEA verification gap widens; Iran maintains 60% U-235 enrichment unmonitored
alerts.summary: UN inspectors unable to verify Iran's enriched uranium inventory since fighting escalated, per Reuters/NYT/MSN nuclear headlines in global_metrics. Iran continues 60% U-235 enrichment with ~1-2 week weapons-grade breakout window. IAEA reports inability to inspect facilities hit in recent strikes, creating critical intelligence blind spot. Iran formally accusing IAEA of politicizing assessments while enrichment continues parallel to active military operations.

alerts.title: US news sentiment darkens sharply; 'Trump,' 'war,' 'talks' dominate discourse
alerts.summary: US sentiment analysis shows negative sentiment spike to 56% (up from prior baseline), with 'Trump' (48 mentions), 'war' (10), and 'talks' (9) as top trending terms. Spike reflects uncertainty over ceasefire durability, missile defense successes, and Trump administration messaging on Iran campaign. Word cloud shift suggests public focus on whether negotiations or further escalation will prevail. Contrast with India's more balanced sentiment (49% positive) indicates US domestic polarization.

analysis_note: The US-Iran ceasefire is fragile and tested hourly by reciprocal strikes, missile interceptions, and proxy escalations; nuclear verification gaps and Iranian enrichment continuation create imminent breakout risk while Trump administration signals both restraint-testing and continued pressure.
